notes: Windows + Cursor CLI (agent) + Ralph method (no upstream bash script). One iteration handles one story.

userStories.title: Define feed-management split model for RSS and favorites collections
userStories.description: Define backend/frontend contract for presenting RSS subscriptions and favorites collections as separate top-level list domains under feed management.
acceptanceCriteria: Data contract supports two list groups: RSS feeds and favorites collections.
API contract and architecture docs document split behavior and compatibility.
Existing RSS storage/fetch behavior remains unchanged.

userStories.title: Implement backend list/query support for split RSS and favorites views
userStories.description: Implement backend read APIs so feed management can request and render RSS subscription list and favorites collection list separately.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend response includes deterministic markers for RSS vs favorites collections.
Normal RSS flow remains backward compatible.
Backend tests cover mixed-data coexistence.

userStories.title: Build feed-management UI with top-level favorites and separate lists
userStories.description: Update feed management page layout and interactions so Article Favorites is a top-level peer of RSS subscriptions, and favorites collections render in their own list block.
acceptanceCriteria: UI shows a top-level favorites entry parallel to RSS subscriptions.
Favorites and RSS are rendered in separate, clearly distinguishable lists.
Navigation from both lists to article list remains stable.

userStories.title: Add frontend and UI automation regression coverage for split feed domains
userStories.description: Add frontend tests and E2E checks for two-list feed management behavior to prevent regressions in RSS and favorites collection interactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests verify RSS and favorites are rendered in separate lists.
Automation verifies favorites top-level entry is visible and usable.
Regression checks ensure existing RSS management still works.

userStories.title: Implement favorites collection article delete API and persistence
userStories.description: Provide backend capability to delete articles from favorites collections and persist deletion safely without impacting other feeds/articles.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend API supports deleting an article from a favorites collection.
Deletion persists across refresh/restart and does not affect unrelated data.
Backend tests cover happy path, missing article, and repeated delete idempotency.

userStories.title: Refine favorites collection article list create action wording and UX
userStories.description: Update favorites collection article list create interaction text and behavior so the button no longer uses "Add custom article" wording and remains intuitive for users.
acceptanceCriteria: Create button text is updated and does not contain "Add custom article".
Create entry remains visible and reachable in favorites article list.
Existing article create form flow remains functional.

userStories.title: Complete favorites collection article create/delete frontend flow
userStories.description: Integrate favorites collection article create and delete operations end-to-end in frontend so users can add and remove articles from the list with immediate feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can create an article and see it appear in favorites list.
Users can delete an article and see persistent removal after refresh/re-entry.
UI shows clear success/failure feedback for create and delete.

userStories.title: Implement one-shot URL autofill state and source-priority merge rules
userStories.description: For URL-based article creation, implement backend rule engine where URL extraction runs only once per create-attempt lifecycle, missing title/content remain empty on extraction failure, creation fails when required fields are still empty, and time field precedence is user input > URL extracted value > default current time.
acceptanceCriteria: URL extraction runs at most once per create-attempt lifecycle.
If title/content are user-empty and extraction fails, fields stay empty and creation is rejected.
Time source precedence is deterministic: user input > URL extracted > default now.
Non-empty user-provided title/content are never overwritten.

userStories.title: Add backend/frontend automation for one-shot URL autofill and failure gating
userStories.description: Add automated tests for one-shot URL extraction behavior, required-field failure gating, and time-source precedence across backend API and frontend create interactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend tests verify one-shot extraction and no re-fetch on repeated submit.
Backend/frontend tests cover create failure when title/content stay empty after extraction.
Tests verify time-source precedence and regressions for URL/no-URL paths.
